name: HubSpot Sales Hub
highlight_text: Category: Scalable CRM for Growing Teams
description:
__POSSIBLE_UNTRANSLATED__

What HubSpot Does

HubSpot Sales Hub is among the most well-known CRMs for scaling teams, and for good reason. It combines sales pipelines, marketing automation, and customer service tools into a single ecosystem.

For agencies, HubSpot’s strength lies in its all-in-one marketing and sales alignment. Teams can capture leads, nurture them through automated workflows, and track every client touchpoint in real time.

Source: HubSpot

Key Features

🏆Deal management tool to track contacts, companies, deals, and tasks.

🏆Email tracking & scheduling to see when prospects open emails or click links and schedule outreach within Gmail or Outlook.

🏆Share sales documents, create proposals, and let clients pick meeting times via integrated calendars.

🏆Live chat & chatbots with real‑time support and lead capture.

🏆Higher‑tier plans include Sales automation & AI. 

Benefits

  • Scalable pricing: The free CRM supports up to 2 users and 1,000 contacts; paid tiers start at an affordable price. 
  • All‑in‑one ecosystem: HubSpot includes marketing, sales, and service hubs.
  • Rich integrations: Native integrations with Gmail, Outlook, Google Calendar, and over 1,400 apps. 

Pricing

  • Free: US $0/month 
  • Starter: US $15 per seat/month for advanced features

Pros

✅ Intuitive UI with scalable functionality

✅ Deep integration with HubSpot Marketing Hub

✅ Excellent automation and analytics

Cons

🚫 Costs rise sharply with additional users or advanced features

🚫 Can feel overwhelming for small agencies

‍
what_people_say:
__POSSIBLE_UNTRANSLATED__

What Customers Say

“I find it incredibly beneficial that HubSpot Sales Hub connects effortlessly to my email and automatically creates leads for me. Additionally, its user-friendly nature makes it easy to navigate, and I appreciate the ability to book meetings seamlessly within the platform.”

– Joshua Foster C.

‍
